div.p: Addressing modes for bytecode branch targets and Instruction Pointer (IP) control.

g.text: Absolute Encoding (3 Bytes: Op, High, Low)
Relative Encoding (3 Bytes: Op, High, Low)
High readability: Disassembler shows actual destination address.
Requires mental math: `IP + offset` to find destination.
Brittle: Code must be patched if moved in memory.
Position-Independent Code (PIC): Jumps valid regardless of load address.
Signed logic: Allows backward jumps (loops) via negative offsets.
Simpler Implementation: No signed arithmetic in IP update.
Moderate complexity: Handle Two's Complement sign extension.

ol.li: Visibility: Easier to trace execution in custom debuggers.
Sign Simplicity: Bytecode operands are treated as unsigned integers.
Alignment: CPython (our reference) utilizes absolute offsets in JUMP_ABSOLUTE instructions.

p.em: Note: For production Shared Objects (.so / .dll), relative offsets are mandatory for PIC support.

div.p: Mechanism The operand is the exact memory address. JUMP 42 → Set IP to 42.
Mechanism The operand is a signed displacement. JUMP +10 → Set IP to (Current + 10).
